scenarios.name: Coffee Shop
scenarios.description: Order coffee and cafe drinks easily
scenarios.tip: Short Thai helps when you need to adjust sweetness, ice, or takeaway quickly at a busy cafe.

scenarios.name: Street Food / Restaurant
scenarios.tip: Street-food Thai should prioritize spice, ice, takeaway, and simple allergy or ingredient repair.

scenarios.name: Ride App / Taxi
scenarios.tip: Showing the map pin helps, but Thailand still needs quick phrases for pickup points, meter use, and stop-here moments.

scenarios.name: Asking the Price
scenarios.tip: Ask the price early and be ready for cash, card, or QR-payment clarification in small shops and markets.

scenarios.name: Polite Basics
scenarios.tip: Thai politeness particles matter, so this scenario should stay near the front of the starter experience.

scenarios.name: Convenience Store
scenarios.tip: Convenience stores are high-frequency traveler stops for water, chargers, tissues, SIM help, and receipts.

scenarios.name: Hotel / Hostel
scenarios.tip: Hotel Thai should cover passports, reservations, deposits, air conditioning, towels, and key-card trouble.

scenarios.name: Directions
scenarios.tip: Directions should bias toward stations, exits, piers, and map-confirmation repair rather than textbook navigation drills.

scenarios.name: Simple Problems
scenarios.tip: This lane should cover misunderstanding, phone/data trouble, getting lost, and first-step medical or police escalation.

scenarios.name: Small Talk
scenarios.tip: Keep Thai small talk short, warm, and optional; it should never crowd out the higher-stress service scenarios.

notes: Shared traveler baseline scaffold for Thai. No scenario-ID deviation from the family 10-scenario seam; Thai-specific changes belong in phrase choice, script/pronunciation handling, and quick-phrase priority before any runtime wiring.
